intro: Athanasius of Alexandria (c. 296–373) was the tireless champion of the full divinity of Christ against Arianism. As a young deacon at Nicaea (325) and then as bishop, he defended the homoousios through five exiles — “Athanasius against the world.” His On the Incarnation remains a classic, and his Festal Letter of 367 lists the twenty-seven books of the New Testament canon. A Doctor of the Church.
